what does the folders tab do in agency tier?

Gemini 3 Flash Preview Ran for s
spark Framing Premium Organization
chevron_right

In the Agency Tier, the Folders tab (or Asset Libraries) serves as a high-level organizational tool designed for professional teams and studios managing multiple clients or large-scale product lines.

Here is exactly what it provides:

  1. Client/Brand Segregation: Unlike the flat "Assets" list in lower tiers, Folders allow you to group specific product bases and logos by brand or project. For example, you can have a "Spring 2025 Collection" folder or a "Client: Acme Corp" folder.

  2. Team Sharing: Assets saved within specific folders can be shared across team accounts (conceptualized for a multi-user SaaS), ensuring everyone uses the latest approved brand assets.

  3. Contextual Studio Loading: When you enter the Studio view, you can "load" a folder, which instantly filters the available assets to only those relevant to that specific project, speeding up the workflow.

  4. Bulk Management: It enables bulk uploading and deleting of assets within a specific category, which is essential for agencies handling hundreds of variations.

In short, it’s the transition from an individual creator tool to a professional production pipeline, allowing for a cleaner, more efficient workspace when handling high-volume output.

can you add those function in and add widgets in the folder tab that explain and provide fields for the user to these things?

Gemini 3 Flash Preview Ran for s
spark Developing Folder Implementation
chevron_right

To fully implement the Agency Tier organizational features, we need to add folder management, asset segregation, and contextual loading.

I will update types.ts to include the new data structures and App.tsx to implement the Folders view and the Studio filtering logic.

Specification

  1. Folder Management:

    • A dedicated Folders view accessible only to Agency users.

    • Capability to create, name, and delete folders (e.g., "Spring 2025 Campaign", "Client: TechGiant").

    • A visual grid of folders showing asset counts.

  2. Asset Segregation:

    • Inside a folder, users can see all assigned products and logos.

    • "Bulk Upload" button within folders that automatically assigns new assets to that project context.

  3. Contextual Studio Loading:

    • A "Load to Studio" action within any folder.

    • When active, the Studio view only displays assets from that specific folder, preventing brand cross-contamination and speeding up the UI.

    • Visual "Project Context" indicator in the top navigation when a folder is loaded.

  4. Team Sharing Widgets:

    • Informational widgets explaining folder-based permissions and shared libraries.

Cherry Product Mockup Visualization
Applying file changes
Send prompt
Add files from Google Drive
Insert files (text, images, audio, video) into your prompt.
Take a picture and insert it into your prompt.
Add files
Update application title to Cherry Mockups.
Rename SKU FOUNDRY to Cherry Mockups throughout the application, update logo icons to Cherry icon.
Update accent color to rose/indigo for consistency with Cherry brand.
Update primary button color to rose for consistency with Cherry Mockups brand.
Add a loading spinner to the asset grid items in AssetSection to improve UX while base64 images decode or render.
Add "Generate Variations" functionality to the gallery. This adds a button to each generated mockup to create 2 additional alternatives using the same configuration but with varied creative instructions (e.g., different lighting or environments).
Add backgroundStyle to GeneratedMockup type.
Update generateMockup to handle background styles and context-aware environment generation.
Add background style selection UI to the Studio view and integrate with generation logic.
Enhance Studio view with dedicated scale and rotation controls for selected logos. Added selectedLayerUid state, visual selection feedback on canvas, and a 'Transform Logo' control panel in the sidebar.
Add 'history' to the AppView union type.
Implement the History view and update navigation. The History view provides a detailed list of all generations with structured metadata like timestamps, layer counts, and background styles.
Request camera permissions for the new Live Studio (Try-On) feature.
Implement the 'Try-On' (Live Studio) view. This feature allows users to use their camera, overlay a logo on real-world objects, and use AI to generate a photorealistic composite mockup of that 'live' interaction.
Add 'landing' to AppView and define PricingPlan type.
Add Landing Page with 3-tier SaaS pricing and subscription logic. Update navigation to support a separate 'front' landing view before entering the application dashboard.
Implement SaaS tiers with specific restrictions for Tier 1 (Starter). Tier 1 now has no background editing, no history, no gallery, no live studio, and a 3-mockup limit. Added visual feedback for locked features and updated the pricing UI.
Update SaaS tiers with specific limits and features. Tier 1: 3 mockups max, no bg edit/history/live/gallery. Tier 2: 10 mockups/mo, full access. Tier 3: Unlimited, custom prompt templates, and asset library folders.
Implement layer reordering in the Studio view with a dedicated "Layers" panel in the sidebar. Users can now drag and drop layers to change their stacking order. Updated Tier 2 limits and refined Tier 3 Agency features including Custom AI Environments and Asset Libraries.
Implement custom background presets for Tier 3 (Agency) users. Added state to manage custom environments, a creation form in the Studio sidebar, and updated the rendering logic to support these user-defined styles. Refined the UI to show saved presets with unique icons in the Studio view.
Add Folder interface to support Agency tier organizational features.
Implement Folders view for Agency tier, including folder creation, asset filtering, and contextual studio loading. Updated Studio view to respect active folder context.
